data.title: Gasoline cracks firm
data.description:
Northwest European gasoline profit margins rose by about 77 cents a barrel on Friday to $16.99.

  • A total of 4,000 metric tons of Eurobob E5 gasoline barges traded as ExxonMobil sold to Trafigura.

  • No Eurobob E10 gasoline barges traded.

  • Russia's fourth-largest oil refinery, the Ryazan plant located south east of Moscow with a 537,000 barrels per day capacity, halted a primary crude distillation unit on Wednesday following a Ukrainian drone attack, two industry sources told Reuters on Friday.

  • Gasoline stocks independently held in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp (ARA) refining and storage hub fell by over 8% in the week to Thursday to 1.04 million tons, data from Dutch consultancy Insights Global showed on Thursday.​

  • EU and UK gasoline exports to other regions have averaged 943,000 bpd in October so far, according to Kpler data, compared with 902,000 bpd in September.

data.title: Santee Cooper in talks to sell inactive reactors to Brookfield, WSJ reports
data.description:
Public utility company Santee Cooper is in advanced talks to sell two inactive nuclear reactors to Brookfield Asset Management that could power data centers underpinning the AI boom, the Wall Street Journal reported on Friday.

The utility's board signed off on Brookfield's proposal, allowing the two parties to enter into exclusive talks to complete terms, the report added. The deal being discussed is expected to be valued in the billions.

In January, the company was seeking buyers for the two partially built nuclear units at the V.C. Summer Nuclear Station in Jenkinsville, South Carolina.

The project was scrapped in July 2017 after two utilities, Santee Cooper and Scana-owned South Carolina Electric and Gas Co (SCE&G), had spent about $9 billion developing it.

Brookfield Asset Management and Santee Cooper did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

data.title: Dutch man to appear in court for death threats to politicians
data.description:
An 18-year old Dutch man is set to appear before a fast-track judge on Monday after he made death threats against two leaders of political parties ahead of a general election, Dutch prosecutors said on Friday.

The man, whose identity was not disclosed, wrote to a politician in a private message on Instagram that if he encountered him, "he would knock him out, drag him into a van, hang him, and cut him open," the prosecutors said.

An investigation into his phone showed he had also threatened to kill another party leader on September 24, the prosecutors said.

Details about the party leaders were not disclosed to protect their privacy, they added.

Many politicians in the Netherlands have reported threats ahead of the October 29 election.

Earlier this week, another man was detained by the police over threats posted on TikTok.

Far right leader Geert Wilders, who has been living under tight security for over 20 years due to death threats, this month briefly suspended his campaign citing a new threat.

data.title: Amazon Sentiment Around AWS Seen as Improving in 3Q — Market Talk
data.description: Amazon shares are set up to gain following its third-quarter financial report, Wedbush analysts say. Investor sentiment has eased since the last quarter's results, with shares down around 3%, as AWS growth was somewhat disappointing compared with expectations, the analysts say. But the analysts think Amazon's long-term opportunity is still there and they expect to hear positive commentary around AWS growth in 3Q. They see multiple areas for margin improvement, including a structural mix shift toward higher-margin AWS and advertising revenues. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

data.title: Russian rouble surges after symbolic key rate cut
data.description:
The Russian rouble strengthened against the U.S. dollar and China's yuan on Friday after the central bank cut its key interest rate by 50 basis points (bps) and raised inflation forecasts for both this year and 2025.

By 1620 GMT, the rouble was up 1.9% at 79.74 against the U.S. dollar in over-the-counter trade, and by almost 2% at 11.15 against the yuan on the Moscow Stock Exchange, where the Chinese unit is the most traded foreign currency.

The small cut, which some analysts called "symbolic", and hawkish wording on inflation in the statement supported the rouble, which benefits from high interest rates since keeping money in rouble-denominated assets becomes more attractive.

The rouble has been strengthening during the week in the run-up to the central bank's meeting despite new Ukraine-related U.S. sanctions that targeted Russian oil companies Lukoil and Rosneft .

Russian Central Bank Governor Elvira Nabiullina told a news conference after the board meeting that shrinking imports and lower demand for foreign currency were the two main factors behind the stronger exchange rate of the rouble.

"We must admit that our exchange rate has become more volatile," she said.

data.title: MOHAWK INDUSTRIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Mohawk Industries Inc., a leading global flooring manufacturer, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, highlighting key metrics and strategic initiatives that have shaped the quarter.

Financial Highlights

Mohawk Industries Inc. reported the following key financial metrics for the third quarter of 2025:

  • Net Sales: $2,757.9 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year due to favorable foreign exchange rates, despite lower sales volume and fewer shipping days.
  • Gross Profit: $654.9 million, a decrease from the previous year primarily due to higher input costs and restructuring expenses, partially offset by productivity gains.
  • Operating Income: $136.7 million, down from the previous year, impacted by higher input costs, legal settlements, and restructuring costs, with some offset from productivity improvements.
  • Net Earnings Attributable to Mohawk Industries, Inc.: $108.8 million, a decrease from the previous year, influenced by higher costs and lower sales volume.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share Attributable to Mohawk Industries, Inc.: $1.75, reflecting the impact of reduced net earnings.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share Attributable to Mohawk Industries, Inc.: $1.75, consistent with basic EPS, indicating no significant dilution effects.

Business Highlights

The company's operational performance for the third quarter of 2025 is summarized as follows:

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The Global Ceramic segment reported net sales of $1,104.7 million, benefiting from favorable foreign exchange rates and a positive price/product mix.
    • Flooring NA saw a decrease in net sales to $936.8 million, primarily due to lower sales volume.
    • Flooring ROW increased to $716.4 million, driven by favorable foreign exchange rates and higher sales volume.
  • Geographical Performance:
    • The United States remains the largest market, contributing significantly to the Global Ceramic and Flooring NA segments.
    • Europe showed strong performance in the Flooring ROW segment, with favorable foreign exchange impacts.
    • Latin America and other regions also contributed positively to the overall sales growth.
  • Sales Units:
    • The company experienced a decrease in sales volume in the Flooring NA segment, impacting overall sales.
    • The Flooring ROW segment saw an increase in sales volume, contributing to its revenue growth.
  • New Production Launches:
    • The company plans to invest approximately $480 million in 2025 to complete capacity expansion projects and targeted initiatives aimed at cost reduction and operational performance improvement.
  • Future Outlook:
    • The company anticipates that restructuring actions and productivity initiatives will deliver annual savings of approximately $50 million, with an estimated cost of $30 million. These projects are expected to streamline operations and enhance future performance, with execution timelines extending into 2026.

SEC Filing: MOHAWK INDUSTRIES INC [ MHK ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.title: HUNT J B TRANSPORT SERVICES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
HUNT J B TRANSPORT SERVICES INC, a leading provider of transportation and logistics services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational achievements, providing insights into its revenue streams, profitability, and strategic initiatives.

Financial Highlights

Total Operating Revenues: $3,052.9 million, a slight decrease of 0.5% from $3,068.2 million in the third quarter of 2024. This decline was primarily due to a reduction in revenue per load in the JBI and JBT segments, lower volumes in ICS and DCS, and fewer stops in FMS.

Operating Income: $242.7 million, an increase of 8.3% from $224.1 million in the third quarter of 2024. This growth was driven by improved network balance and efficiency in JBI and higher productivity in DCS.

Net Earnings: $170.8 million, up 12.4% from $152.1 million in the third quarter of 2024. The increase reflects higher operating income and a decrease in net interest expense.

Basic Earnings Per Share: $1.78, up from $1.50 in the third quarter of 2024, reflecting the increase in net earnings.

Diluted Earnings Per Share: $1.76, up from $1.49 in the third quarter of 2024, due to improved profitability and share repurchases.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments

  • Intermodal (JBI): Revenue decreased by 2% to $1.52 billion, primarily due to a 1% decrease in load volumes and revenue per load.
  • Dedicated Contract Services (DCS): Revenue increased by 2% to $864 million, driven by a 3% increase in productivity.
  • Integrated Capacity Solutions (ICS): Revenue decreased by 1% to $276 million, with an 8% decrease in overall volumes but a 9% increase in revenue per load.
  • Final Mile Services (FMS): Revenue decreased by 5% to $206 million.
  • Truckload (JBT): Revenue increased by 10% to $190 million.

Sales Units

  • JBI's load volume decreased by 1% in Q3 2025, with transcontinental loads down 6% and eastern network load volume up 6%.
  • DCS's average truck count decreased by 1%, while JBT's load volume increased by 14%.

Operational Efficiency

  • JBI improved network balance, resulting in fewer empty container moves and increased efficiency in the drayage fleet.
  • DCS saw improvements in productivity due to contractual rate increases and reduced idle equipment.
  • ICS improved its operating loss position due to lower personnel costs and reduced technology expenses.

Future Outlook

  • The company expects to continue focusing on network balance and cost management initiatives to drive operational efficiency.
  • The annual tax rate for 2025 is expected to be approximately 24.5%.
  • The company plans to spend between $550 million to $600 million on net capital expenditures for the full year 2025.

Geographical Performance

  • JBI's eastern network load volume increased by 6%, highlighting strong performance in that region, while transcontinental loads decreased by 6%, reflecting strategic decisions to prioritize network balance.

Cost Management

  • The company has implemented initiatives to lower the cost to serve across segments, contributing to improved operating income in JBI and DCS, despite challenges in other areas such as insurance claims and maintenance costs.

SEC Filing: HUNT J B TRANSPORT SERVICES INC [ JBHT ] - 10-Q - Oct. 24, 2025

data.description:
RNS Number : 8266E European Smaller Companies Tst PLC 24 October 2025  

JANUS HENDERSON FUND MANAGEMENT UK LIMITED

THE EUROPEAN SMALLER COMPANIES TRUST PLC

LEGAL ENTITY IDENTIFIER: 213800N1B1HCQG2W4V90

24 October 2025

THE EUROPEAN SMALLER COMPANIES TRUST PLC

(the 'Company')

Market purchase by the Company of its own shares

Notification is given that, pursuant to the authority granted at the Annual General Meeting of the Company held on 25 November 2024 to make market purchases of the Company's own ordinary shares up to a maximum of 14.99%, a market purchase of 21,009 ordinary shares in the capital of the Company was made today at a price of 212.12p per share. These shares will be held in treasury.

Following the above purchase, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will continue to be 410,375,045 ordinary shares of 1.5625p each, of which 52,962,542 (12.91% of the issued share capital) are held in treasury and have no voting rights.

Holders of the Company's ordinary shares are entitled to one vote for every share held. As treasury shares have no voting rights, the total number of voting rights in the Company is now 357,412,503.  Shareholders should use this figure for notifications in keeping with the provisions of DTR 5.

For further information, please call:

data.title: Mohawk slips after forecasting fourth-quarter profit below estimates
data.description:
** Shares of home flooring manufacturer Mohawk Industries fall 5% to $122 in early afternoon trading

** Co expects Q4 profit below estimates on weak ceramic and flooring sales

** MHK estimates Q4 adj. profit between $1.90 and $2.00 per share, below the estimate of $2.14 apiece, according to data compiled by LSEG

** Co posts Q3 adj. profit of $2.67/share, beating analysts' avg. estimate of $2.64/share

** Reports quarterly revenue of $2.76 bln, above estimate of $2.73 bln

** Including session moves, shares up 3.2% YTD
